questions.q: Identify the characteristic feature of tertiary activities?
What is the primary sector of economy and give an example of it?
questions.answer: The primary sector of the economy involves the extraction of raw materials. An example of this is mining.

questions.q: What is the main difference between tertiary and secondary activities?
questions.answer: The primary difference between tertiary and secondary activities is that secondary activities involve the production of goods, whereas tertiary activities involve the provision of services.

questions.q: Identify the sector of the economy which is the primary source of employment and income for the majority of the population.
What is the term used to describe the transformation of raw materials into finished goods?
Explain the concept of tertiary activities and provide an example.
questions.answer: Tertiary activities refer to the provision of services. An example of this is the banking sector, where financial services are provided to people and businesses.

questions.q: Distinguish between primary and secondary activities.
questions.answer: Primary activities involve the extraction of raw materials, while secondary activities involve the transformation of raw materials into finished goods.

questions.q: What is the difference between the manufacturing sector and the service sector?
questions.answer: The manufacturing sector involves the transformation of raw materials into finished goods, whereas the service sector involves the provision of services.

questions.q: Identify the sector of the economy that involves the provision of services.
Explain the significance of the tertiary sector in the economy.
questions.answer: The tertiary sector is significant in the economy as it provides employment and income opportunities for a large number of people. It also contributes to the country's GDP.
